title: At the bank
setting: You've waited in line at the bank. It's your turn at the teller's window.

steps.speaker: Cajero
steps.es: Buenos días. ¿En qué le puedo servir?
steps.en: Good morning. How can I help you?

options.es: Quisiera abrir una cuenta, por favor.
options.en: I'd like to open an account, please.
options.feedback: 'Quisiera' (I'd like) is the polished, formal way to ask. Perfect for a bank.
Way too blunt for a formal setting — and 'dame' is too casual here.
Understandable, just abrupt. A full polite sentence fits the bank better.

steps.speaker: Cajero
steps.es: Con gusto. ¿Trae algún documento de identidad?
steps.en: Gladly. Do you have an ID document?

options.feedback: Direct and prepared. Exactly what he needs to proceed.
A smart question if unsure — better to ask than to be turned away.
You can't open an account with no ID. Always bring documents to the bank.

steps.speaker: Cajero
steps.es: Perfecto. Llene este formulario y firme abajo.
steps.en: Perfect. Fill out this form and sign below.

options.feedback: Asking to be sure before signing anything official is exactly the right instinct.
Fine if you're sure — but on official forms, confirming where never hurts.

options.en: I don't like filling out forms.
options.feedback: Relatable! But it's required — best to just work through it politely.

steps.speaker: Cajero
steps.es: Todo en orden. Su cuenta estará activa mañana.
steps.en: Everything's in order. Your account will be active tomorrow.

options.en: Thank you very much for your help.
options.feedback: Polite and warm — the right note to end a formal interaction on.
Next-day activation is normal. Complaining about it just sours things.
Smooth and courteous. A good-day wish is always welcome.
